text:
Over the years, there has been a dramatic improvement in optical sensor technology that makes the processing of high resolution optical data a commonplace in a vast number of applications. Currently, these applications make use of 4K and 8K ultra-high-definition (UHD) content and therefore pose significant processing challenges to modern image processing algorithms. This work explores the low-light image enhancement (LLIE) challenges associated with high resolution ultra-high definition 4K and 8K content processing. We first introduce a new benchmark dataset known as the ultra-high definition large optical lighting UHD-LOL dataset and subsequently use this new dataset for a through investigation and benchmarking of contemporary LLIE approaches.

High-resolution image and video enhancement is increasingly becoming a computational bottle- neck. In this work, we introduce a new transformer-based model named LLFormer to efficiently enlarge the scale of input images and videos. The key components of our model are an axis-based multi-head self-attention (A-MHSA) mechanism and a cross-layer attention fusion block. By doing so, our model achieves linear complexity w.r.t. the input length, while still maintains the long-range relationships in the data. We evaluate our model on both our constructed UHD-LOL dataset, as well as on the widely used LOL and MIT-Adobe FiveK datasets. The results clearly show that LLFormer achieves the state-of-the-art performance. We also show that our model can serve as a very useful pre-processing step for other tasks, such as face detection, especially in low light conditions. The source code and pre-trained models are available at https://github.com/TaoWangzj/LLFormer.
